name: elevated_release_place

symptoms: Tilted-axis place drags the object several cm along the lean direction; final placement error 70-190 mm
Object's final offset vector matches the horizontal lean direction of the approach axis
trace_evidence: Descending the held object all the way to surface height means object contacts table while gripper keeps moving along tilted axis
Table friction drags the object in the lean direction as the gripper continues its tilted descent
Offset direction is consistent with the horizontal component of the tilted approach vector

when_to_apply: Any place motion that uses a tilted or angled approach axis (not purely vertical), especially when combined with a mobile base at a non-default station (see station_relative_approach_dir).
repair_strategy: Stop the place descent 1.5–2.5 cm ABOVE the target surface height, open the gripper there (short free-fall drop of ~15-25 mm), then retreat along the approach axis. Also ensure the place approach direction is computed from the actual live arm-base position (see station_relative_approach_dir skill) rather than a hardcoded origin.
code_sketch: # Descend to hover height, NOT to surface
# Open gripper and let object drop
wait_steps(8)  # ~0.13 s at 60 Hz — object settles
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro (ManiSkill GPU sim) 2026-07-01: place error reduced from 73 mm with surface-contact descent; rerun with elevated release pending (expected <60 mm)
